campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and the overall treasurer's report. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to better connect different types of transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and money management
City treasurer's financial report for July
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - connecting buses, biking, walking, and other transit options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice opinions on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public comments, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city rule) and skipping the standard first review process to speed things up. The council directed staff to put this ordinance on the fast-track agenda for final approval at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite approval process
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote at next regular council meeting
Staff directed to place item on consent calendar (fast-track agenda)

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to finalize their weed control efforts for 2026 and establish new rules for how the council conducts meetings. They also approved plans to fly the Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags, discussed affordable housing policies, and heard an update from the regional transit authority about transportation.
keyTopics: Completion of 2026 weed abatement program
New official procedures for City Council meetings
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Flying Pride and Juneteenth flags at City Hall
Regional transportation authority presentation and housing element updates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with various projects and services. The council authorized the town manager to sign deals with construction and professional services companies, join a statewide insurance program, and handle an art transfer agreement.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance coverage
Multiple contract amendments with existing service providers
Art transfer agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving children and young people through various programs and services. They discussed updates on youth programs like the Youth Empowerment Alliance and a summer safety initiative, checked on how code enforcement is doing its job, and looked at plans to improve library buildings and customer service.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer safety programs for kids
Code Enforcement performance review
Library facility improvements and customer experience upgrades
Youth intervention and support services update

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in person and online. The meeting provided instructions for virtual attendees to join via Zoom or phone and submit public comments.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Phone dial-in option (669-900-9128)
How to raise hand to speak during agenda items

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the minutes from their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three commissioners about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission regular business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a food and budget program, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and job opportunities for young people through a summer jobs program. The council also approved minutes from their previous March meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any city matters for up to three minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer jobs opportunities for young people
Approval of previous meeting minutes (March 11, 2026)
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 where residents could participate both in-person and remotely. The city is making it easy for people to join by offering Zoom access and phone dial-in options, plus the ability to submit written comments online before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - in-person and remote participation options available
Zoom meeting access for residents unable to attend in person
Phone dial-in option (1-669-900-6833) for those without video access
Online eComment submission process for written public comments

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several administrative and financial matters, including joining a state tax program with the county, deciding on fee waivers for a community education organization, and awarding contracts for construction work. The council also reviewed plans to update the city's housing and community development strategy and discussed new project requests from individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining state Franchise Tax Board program with county
Fee waiver decision for Milpitas Community Educational organization
Construction contract award and project approval
Updates to city's 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests from council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss the city's gas utility finances and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision was whether to approve changes to how much residents and businesses pay for natural gas service, along with adjustments to the utility's financial reserves.
keyTopics: Gas rate changes for residential customers (Schedule G-1)
Gas rate changes for commercial and large business customers (Schedules G-2 and G-3)
Fiscal Year 2027 gas utility budget and financial forecast
Transfer of money between utility reserves and the city's general fund
